message: Stage -1.2 drafted angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start runner=codex/gpt-5.5 status=completed
Stage 0 split completed (attempt 1): law-sharp=confirmed[salvaged:A], critical-l=confirmed, plugin-inference=confirmed

NL artifact written: needs-new-infrastructure with 4 extracted in-scope T-blocks (joint envelope, critical-L monotonicity/zero-contact/LP-value, deterministic relaxed-LP containment, mean-completion image+nesting+necessity); 3 Defer items extracted.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 halted (below novelty floor, not salvageable): [D0.5.G cold referee] delivered tier=subfield below novelty_target=field (floor=field). The delivered result is a finite-dimensional LP formulation whose main sharpness step becomes a direct two-point mean-completion construction once the missing inequalities are added. The advertised law-sharp interval is sharp only for a very permissive completion class with arbitrary off-arm product kernels, so the causal content is much narrower than the continuous-treatment DiD headline suggests. The critical-L theorem is standard parametric LP basis sensitivity, and the inference theorem gives conservative pointwise outer coverage by enlarging the feasible set with diverging padding rather than a sharp or broadly usable inference theory. This is a real but specialized algebraic partial-identification note, not a field-level contribution.
